codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all 12 expected behaviors. 11 files changed (+1668 lines), 7 new files plus 4 modifications. ViewModel follows @MainActor @Observable pattern with proper DI. Views use liquid glass theme consistently. 28 unit tests with good coverage of ViewModel logic, save/load round-trip, and edge cases. EnumsTests fix is correct. HeatmapVisualization.displayName properly added to shared NetMonitorCore package.

issues.description: MeasurementMarker uses UIScreen.main.bounds for offset calculation. UIScreen.main is deprecated since iOS 16 and unreliable in multi-scene contexts. Should use the containerSize passed from the parent GeometryReader instead.

rssiColor computed property duplicates the same RSSI-to-Color threshold logic found in MeasurementDetailSheet.swift:168. Should be extracted to a shared helper (e.g., Theme extension or utility function). Also, the first two branches (rssi >= -50 and rssi >= -65) both return Theme.Colors.success, making the -50 branch redundant.

signalIconName has redundant branches: rssi >= -50 and rssi >= -65 both return 'wifi'. The -50 branch is unreachable dead code since anything >= -50 would match >= -65 first if ordered differently, and both return the same value regardless.

renderHeatmapOverlay() runs synchronously on the main actor. For large point sets, HeatmapRenderer.render() could cause UI jank. Consider moving rendering to a background task with a MainActor hop back for the assignment. Acceptable for current expected usage (3-50 points).

sharedStateObservations.observation: RSSI-to-Color mapping logic (threshold-based color selection for signal quality) is duplicated identically in FloatingRSSIHUD and MeasurementDetailSheet. A similar pattern exists for signal quality labels. AGENTS.md has no guidance on extracting shared UI helpers within view directories. As heatmap views grow, this duplication will spread.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FloatingRSSIHUD.swift:81-93 and MeasurementDetailSheet.swift:168-180 contain identical rssiColor computed properties. Same for signalQualityLabel in MeasurementDetailSheet:182-192.

sharedStateObservations.observation: MeasurementMarker uses deprecated UIScreen.main.bounds for layout calculations. AGENTS.md and coding conventions don't mention deprecated API avoidance policy. This pattern could spread to other views.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: HeatmapSurveyView.swift:353-354 uses UIScreen.main.bounds.width and UIScreen.main.bounds.height. UIScreen.main deprecated in iOS 16.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from TDD red phase (Step 2) by building ViewModel and views first, then adding tests. The justification ('many interrelated components needed to compile together first') is reasonable for a feature creating 7 new files with cross-dependencies. The skill's TDD requirement may be impractical for large multi-file features where types reference each other.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.deviations[0]: Step 2 TDD Red Phase — worker built implementation first. Transcript confirms ViewModel, views, and tests were created in sequence rather than tests-first.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker ran swift test locally instead of SSH to mac-mini (Step 6). This is a known environment issue documented in AGENTS.md ('mac-mini git HTTPS authentication broken'). The skill procedure should codify the local fallback explicitly rather than relying on workers knowing about the known issue.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.deviations[1]: Step 6 SSH tests — ran swift test locally. AGENTS.md documents this as a known pre-existing issue.

summary: Feature passes review. Implementation covers all 12 expected behaviors: tap-to-measure with blue markers, heatmap overlay after 3+ points at 70% opacity, floating RSSI HUD at 1Hz, 3 visualization types (no SNR per iOS limitation), save/load to .netmonsurvey, point inspection and deletion, spacing guidance, liquid glass theme throughout, Wi-Fi disconnect detection. EnumsTests.swift correctly updated from 18 to 19. 28 well-structured unit tests. Four non-blocking issues found: deprecated UIScreen.main usage, duplicated RSSI color logic, redundant signal icon branches, and synchronous rendering on main thread. None affect correctness for current usage patterns.
